steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
Before the blank, “because of the country's millions of miles of” is an incomplete prepositional phrase that requires a noun phrase as its object.
After the blank, “the volume and speed of water at any given location can vary drastically over time” is a finite clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Commas should not precede a coordinating conjunction when it joins two noun phrases.

Choice D — waterways and the fact that uses “waterways and the fact that” with no commas, correctly joining the two objects of the preposition “because of.” Choices A, B, and C incorrectly insert commas before or after “and,” violating the rule that punctuation should not separate these coordinated noun phrases.

That matches Choice D — waterways and the fact that.
